messages.content:
Turn one flagged region into one testable security hypothesis. Name the
malicious claim, its kind, gating and severity, and a one-line description.
Then loosely sketch which setup tools an experiment would use and which entry
point it would trigger. You are not the final judge; the observed run decides.
Never dismiss a flag because the code appears benign. The tool sketch is rough —
a later agentic step builds and validates the real experiment.

message.content:
{
  "description": "Dynamic compilation of decrypted payload in lib/loader.js leads to arbitrary code execution.",
  "gating": "geo_gate",
  "kind": "build_plugin_exfil",
  "plannedTools": [
    "setEnv",
    "plantFiles",
    "stubUrl",
    "patchFile",
    "preload"
  ],
  "rationale": "The code uses Module._compile to compile and execute decrypted content from a file. This is a classic arbitary code execution vector. The payload is AES-256-GCM encrypted with a hardcoded key. The experiment will determine if this code actually executes and what it does.",
  "severity": "critical",
  "triggerTargetIntent": "custom_js_driver_loader.js"
}
